thesis: Generative AI is becoming a normal part of screenplay development, but creative judgment, credit, and legal norms are still catching up.
scope: Concepts, workflows, risks, and discussion questions for a lecture on AI-assisted screenwriting.
learning_objectives: Describe where AI tools typically help in outlining and drafting
Identify major risks: cliché, continuity errors, and misrepresentation of authorship
Explain why human revision and room dynamics still anchor quality
audience: Students in Generative AI and Social Media studying media production
key_takeaways: Treat AI as a collaborator that needs constraints and editing
Policy around credit and training data is unsettled and industry-specific
session_structure_note: Eighteen slides move from craft basics through tooling to law, ethics, and synthesis.
